From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian music television series

| *Music Album* | |
| --- | --- |
| Genre | music |
| Country of origin | Canada |
| Original language | English |
| No. of seasons | 1 |
| Production | |
| Producer | Neil Andrews |
| Running time | 30 minutes |
| Original release | |
| Network | [CBC Television](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television") |
| Release | 17 September 1970 (1970-09-17) – 28 June 1971 (1971-06-28) |

***Music Album*** was a [Canadian](/wiki/Television_in_Canada "Television in Canada") music television series which aired on [CBC Television](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television") from 1970 to 1971.

## Premise

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Music_Album_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Premise")]

This series featured Canadian musical performers in the popular, light classic and show tune genres. Toronto-produced episodes featured [Lucio Agostini](/wiki/Lucio_Agostini "Lucio Agostini") conducting the show orchestra while the conductor for the Vancouver-produced episodes was [Ricky Hyslop](/wiki/Ricky_Hyslop "Ricky Hyslop"). Wally Koster was a frequent guest artist and [Howard Cable](/wiki/Howard_Cable "Howard Cable") was a guest conductor.[[1]](#cite_note-CCF-1)

## Scheduling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Music_Album_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Scheduling")]

This half-hour series was broadcast Thursdays at 9:30 p.m. (Eastern) from its debut on 17 September 1970. On 4 February 1971, *Music Album* moved to a Thursday 10:00 p.m. time slot, then finally a Monday 7:30 p.m. time slot from 24 May until its concluding episode on 28 June 1971.

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Music_Album_(TV_series)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-CCF_1-0)** Corcelli, John (August 2005). ["Music Album"](http://www.broadcasting-history.ca/programming/television/programming_popup.php?id=1238). Canadian Communications Foundation. Retrieved 7 May 2010.
